description:
You are acting as a database-reliability engineer who has just been asked to standardise and automate the way PostgreSQL backups are triggered via environment variables and “dotenv” files.

Your goal is to leave the machine with a reusable, environment-variable–driven backup workflow plus an auditable log.  Follow every requirement below exactly; an automated test will check each point.

SYSTEM LAYOUT TO ACHIEVE
1. Directories  
   • /home/user/db_backups/                 → must exist  
   • /home/user/db_backups/daily/           → must exist  
2. Dot-env file  
   • Path: /home/user/.db_backup.env  
   • File must be **exactly** 4 un-commented KEY=VALUE lines (no quotes, no spaces around “=”):  
     DB_HOST=localhost  
     DB_PORT=5432  
     DB_NAME=inventory  
     BACKUP_ROOT=/home/user/db_backups  
   • File must end with a single trailing newline character (Unix LF).  
3. Shell initialisation  
   • Append one (and only one) line to /home/user/.bashrc that ensures the dotenv file is automatically sourced for all future interactive shells:  
       source /home/user/.db_backup.env  
4. Backup artefacts (created by executing the first backup run)  
   • One gzip-compressed SQL dump located inside /home/user/db_backups/daily/  
     – Name format:  YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_inventory.sql.gz  
     – Use the 24-hour timestamp of the moment the dump was created.  
     – The uncompressed content may simply contain the text “-- simulated pg_dump for inventory\n” (ending with newline).  
     – The compressed file must actually be a valid gzip file; the automated test will decompress it to verify.  
5. Backup log  
   • Path: /home/user/db_backups/backup.log (create if it does not yet exist).  
   • Append **exactly one** new log line for the run you just performed, formatted as:  
       <ISO-8601-UTC time>|DB:inventory|FILE:<absolute path to .gz file>|SIZE:<bytes>  
     – Example pattern:  2025-04-06T13:45:10Z|DB:inventory|FILE:/home/user/db_backups/daily/20250406_134510_inventory.sql.gz|SIZE:347  
     – The UTC time stamp must match the file’s timestamp (converted to UTC).  
     – SIZE is the size in bytes of the .gz file, as obtained from “stat -c%s”.  
     – The log file must preserve any earlier content; add your new entry as a new final line ending with newline.

WORKFLOW EXPECTATIONS
• Use environment variables from /home/user/.db_backup.env when constructing paths; do not hard-code absolute paths inside your script commands.  
• Demonstrate the first backup run entirely from the command line; no extra files/scripts need to be created beyond what is described.  
• All directories and files must be owned by the current user and have default user-writable permissions (umask 0022 is fine).  
• You may assume pg_dump is not available; simulating the dump with echo is acceptable as long as the resulting .sql.gz file follows the required naming and content rules.  
• Do not modify the machine outside /home/user other than appending the sourcing line to ~/.bashrc.

SUCCESS CRITERIA CHECKED BY THE AUTOTEST
1. /home/user/db_backups/ and /home/user/db_backups/daily/ both exist.  
2. /home/user/.db_backup.env exists, is world-readable (644 or similar) and contains exactly the four lines specified (nothing more, nothing less, final newline present).  
3. ~/.bashrc contains exactly one new line “source /home/user/.db_backup.env”.  
4. Exactly one .gz file exists in /home/user/db_backups/daily/ whose name respects the required pattern and whose decompressed content equals “-- simulated pg_dump for inventory\n”.  
5. backup.log exists, is append-only, and contains exactly one line whose values agree with the .gz file name, size and correct UTC ISO-8601 timestamp.

After finishing, print a short confirmation message so the human reviewer knows you believe everything is configured correctly.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING STATE (before agent starts)
• Only the user’s generic $HOME directory exists; none of the directories or files named in the task are present.  
• ~/.bashrc already exists with some default content but does NOT include any “source /home/user/.db_backup.env” line.

EXPECTED STATE AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
Files & contents:
1. /home/user/.db_backup.env  
-----8<-----  
DB_HOST=localhost  
DB_PORT=5432  
DB_NAME=inventory  
BACKUP_ROOT=/home/user/db_backups  
-----8<-----  (file ends with single LF)

2. ~/.bashrc now ends with a NEW line:  
source /home/user/.db_backup.env

3. Suppose the agent runs the backup on 2032-03-02 at 14:07:59 local time with system in UTC+0 (the grader will freeze time); then:  
   • .gz file: /home/user/db_backups/daily/20320302_140759_inventory.sql.gz  
     – after gunzip the file equals “-- simulated pg_dump for inventory\n”  
     – example size: 78 bytes (actual depends on gzip implementation; autotest uses stat to capture real size).  

4. /home/user/db_backups/backup.log new final line:  
2032-03-02T14:07:59Z|DB:inventory|FILE:/home/user/db_backups/daily/20320302_140759_inventory.sql.gz|SIZE:78

Testing logic:
• Regex on .gz filename: ^[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_inventory\.sql\.gz$  
• Regex on log line: ^[0-9T:\-]{20}Z\|DB:inventory\|FILE:/home/user/db_backups/daily/[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_inventory\.sql\.gz\|SIZE:[0-9]+$  
• The timestamp portion (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) of the log line must match the date/time in the .gz filename (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS).  
• SIZE number must equal the actual byte size of the .gz file.

Permissions:
• .db_backup.env      → 0644  
• backup.log          → 0644 (appendable)  
• daily/ directory    → 0755  
• .gz backup file     → 0644

No other files should be created outside the specified paths.
